title: DAC 2026: Certus Semiconductor Brings Two New I/O Libraries to GlobalFoundries 12nm - Semiwiki
gist: Certus Semiconductor is bringing a full-featured commercial I/O library (GH12) and a dedicated radiation-hardened I/O library for space/defense applications to GlobalFoundries' 12LP and 12LP+ processes, both progressing to tapeout in 2026, positioning GF12 as a node of choice for FPGAs, ASICs, and SoCs needing a long-lived foundry ecosystem.

insights.claim: Certus Semiconductor's GH12 commercial I/O library for GlobalFoundries 12LP/12LP+ operates at 0.8V core and 1.8V/3.3V I/O, meets 2kV HBM ESD with latch-up resistance, covers -40°C to 125°C industrial range, and requires no special ESD implants beyond the base GF12 process, with both the commercial and radiation-hardened libraries progressing to tapeout in 2026.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A 2026 tapeout timeline for both commercial and radiation-hardened 12nm I/O libraries is a concrete milestone for when GlobalFoundries 12LP-based designs (from commercial ASIC/SoC teams to space/defense programs) will have access to Certus's IP for actual production designs.
insights.quote: GH12 operates at 0.8V core and 1.8V/3.3V I/O, meets 2kV HBM ESD with latch-up resistance, covers the full -40°C to 125°C industrial range, and requires no special ESD implants beyond the base GF12 process.

insights.claim: Certus is also delivering a dedicated radiation-hardened-by-design I/O library for both standard 1.8V-3.3V GPIO and FPGA applications on GlobalFoundries 12LP/12LP+, targeting space and defense programs where I/O reliability under radiation is non-negotiable, pairing high-speed differential signaling with wide-range high-voltage I/O and integrated on-chip supply regulation.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A radiation-hardened I/O library specifically for GF12LP targets the space/defense ASIC and FPGA market, a niche but high-value IP segment where few vendors compete, relevant for assessing Certus's addressable market beyond commercial SoC I/O.
insights.quote: Certus is also delivering a dedicated radiation-hardened-by-design I/O library for both Standard 1.8V to 3.3V GPIO Applications as well as FPGA applications on Global 12LP and Global 12LP+, targeting space and defense programs where I/O reliability under radiation is non-negotiable.
